text:
After a terrible storm, a family dared each other to climb the tallest oak tree in their backyard. They gathered by the trunk, limbs gnarled like ancient limbs.

Mrs. Jenkins shouted triumphantly, "Remember Aunt Margaret? Last time you climbed up you fell halfway down! Keep your head!"

Her son Harry grinned, his eyes gleaming with mischief. "I mean, just look at that hawk perched up there! It's definitely looking down."

Lola watched their anticipation. "I wish I could do it," she sighed.

Harry laughed heartily. "You're too cute! You've climbed up that pine twice!"

Their pet dog barked excitedly. They could barely hear him over the boy's laughter.

Harry scooped up a branch and started a daring climb up the side. Lola squealed, surprised at how high they went. They reached a platform broad at the bottom. They sat there for ages.

"We've broken every law of nature," muttered Harry as they
